title: O Lord, Our Lord, in All the Earth

verses.text: From infants’ and from children’s lips You ordered praise to sound To silence all your enemies, The wicked to confound. St. 1-4, 6-7 The Book of Psalms for Worship, 2009,
When I regard the heav’ns you made, Your fingers’ work I trace; I see the moon and shining stars Which you have set in place. St. 1-4, 6-7 The Book of Psalms for Worship, 2009,
I ask myself, “What then is man that you should give him thought – It he son of man, that you to him Such gracious care have brought?” St. 1-4, 6-7 The Book of Psalms for Worship, 2009,
You made him little less than those Who dwell in heav’n above, And you have crowned and honored him With glory from above. St. 1-4, 6-7 The Book of Psalms for Worship, 2009,
You made him ruler of the works Created by your hand; You placed all things beneath his feet To be in his command. St. 1-4, 6-7 The Book of Psalms for Worship, 2009,
